enrichmentScore.reasoning: The assistant created aspects for several life areas (school/work/health/social) and also added course/employer aspects, but it failed to create any recurring events for the user’s stated schedules (classes, gym, runs, study group, problem-set blocks). It also appears to have tool/flow issues (many update_profile calls, no recurring events, and one-time events with incorrect/irrelevant items), indicating anti-patterns like under-tooling for recurring schedules and calendar population failure.

scheduleScore.reasoning: All suggestions are goal-linked and generally explain why they matter, but the resulting schedule data shows major conflicts/invalidity: there are no placement slots, and the one-time events include study/work items that overlap with existing class/work patterns (e.g., CS 161/CS 221 times aren’t respected) and even include odd times (e.g., Problem Sets 02:00–05:00). Because the system’s actual calendar placement cannot be validated and appears to violate the no-overlap, gap, and time-of-day energy rules, the schedule quality is very poor despite decent description quality.

conversationScore.reasoning: The assistant frequently used the wrong or missing tools (e.g., schedule queries missing list_events/search_events, free-time missing find_free_time, modification missing search_events, and duplicate-check missing list_events). It also showed duplicate/overlap risk by creating events without properly searching for existing ones, and some responses appear inconsistent with the current calendar state (e.g., time shifts/aspect handling). Overall behavior matches several anti-patterns: WRONG TOOL usage, UNDER-TOOLING for schedule/free-time, and insufficient duplicate prevention.

character.name: Marcus Chen

goals: Run a half marathon in October

character.personality: You are Marcus Chen, a 22-year-old CS junior at Stanford. You are friendly but concise -- you answer questions directly without over-explaining. You sometimes use casual language like "yeah" and "nah". You volunteer schedule details when asked but don't dump everything at once. You're a bit stressed about balancing school and work but generally upbeat.
enrichmentDetails: I take CS 161 (Algorithms) MWF 10:30am-11:20am in Gates B01 with Professor Roughgarden
I take CS 221 (AI) TTh 1:30pm-2:50pm in Hewlett 200 with Professor Liang
I take MATH 120 (Groups and Rings) MWF 1:30pm-2:20pm in room 380-380C
I work at Blue Bottle Coffee on campus, Tuesday and Thursday mornings 6am-10am, and Saturday 7am-1pm
I go to the gym (lifting) Monday, Wednesday, Friday around 7:30am before class. Usually takes about an hour
I run on Tuesday and Thursday evenings around 6pm, training for a half marathon
I have a study group for CS 161 on Sunday afternoons from 2-5pm at Green Library
I try to do problem sets on weekday evenings, usually 7-10pm
I wake up around 6:30am on weekdays and go to bed around midnight
I play pickup basketball with friends on Saturday afternoons

enrichmentConversation.userMessage: Hey! I just signed up. I'm Marcus.
enrichmentConversation.agentResponse: Nice to meet you, Marcus. What’s the next life area you want to set up first: work, health, relationships, routines, learning, or something else?

enrichmentConversation.userMessage: Hey Glyde, I’m Marcus—yeah, glad to be here. My days are pretty school-heavy: I’m in CS 161 MWF 10:30–11:20am (Gates B01) and CS 221 TTh 1:30–2:50pm (Hewlett 200). I also work at Blue Bottle on campus Tuesday/Thursday mornings 6–10am and Saturday 7am–1pm, so mornings can get busy fast.
enrichmentConversation.agentResponse: Got it, Marcus. I’ve set up your school and work areas; what should we cover next: health, relationships, routines, learning, or something else?

enrichmentConversation.userMessage: Yeah, mornings are definitely the busiest part for me—Tuesday and Thursday I’m at Blue Bottle 6–10am before heading to CS 221 at 1:30pm. On top of that, I’ve got CS 161 MWF 10:30–11:20am in Gates B01 with Prof Roughgarden, so I’m usually in class most of the day. Outside class, I try to keep weekday evenings for problem sets, usually 7–10pm.
enrichmentConversation.agentResponse: Thanks, Marcus. I’ve got your class and work schedule noted, plus your weekday evening problem-set block. What’s the next life area you want to set up: health, relationships, learning, or routines?

enrichmentConversation.userMessage: Yeah, that’s pretty accurate—Tuesday/Thursday mornings are the crunch since I’m at Blue Bottle 6–10am and then CS 221 1:30–2:50pm. I usually do gym lifting around 7:30am on Monday/Wednesday/Friday before class, so mornings stay pretty packed but manageable. Weekday evenings I’m pretty locked in for problem sets from ~7–10pm, since that’s when I can focus best.
enrichmentConversation.agentResponse: Got it, Marcus. I’ve got your mornings and evening focus blocks noted, plus your Blue Bottle shifts. What’s the next life area you want to set up: health, relationships, learning, or routines?

tasks.title: Buy birthday gift for mom
tasks.description: Pick out and buy a birthday gift for mom.

goals.title: Run a half marathon in October

suggestions.title: Budget check-in: track spending to stay on track for $2000
suggestions.description: To save $2000 by summer, you need tight feedback loops. This session updates your budget/spending tracker, identifies any overspend categories, and sets a concrete adjustment for the next 1–2 weeks.

suggestions.title: CS 161 problem-set deep work (full solve + checks)
suggestions.description: Your calendar is packed with Problem Sets, and your goal of a 3.8 GPA this semester depends on consistent, high-quality practice. Use this session to fully solve the next CS 161 problem set (or the hardest subset), then do a final pass to verify edge cases and correctness.

suggestions.title: Run/walk endurance session (easy pace)
suggestions.description: For your half-marathon goal, you’ll progress fastest with consistent aerobic base. This session is an easy-pace endurance workout (run/walk intervals if needed) to build stamina without overtaxing you.

suggestions.title: Half-marathon plan: choose training schedule + baseline run
suggestions.description: Your goal to run a half marathon in October needs a concrete plan. In this session, pick a training schedule (based on your current fitness), set weekly mileage targets, and do a baseline run plan (distance/time) you can start this week.

suggestions.title: GPA boost: 60-min CS review + error log
suggestions.description: To raise your GPA this semester, convert recent work into a reusable system. Review what you got wrong on recent problems, write a short error log (mistake type → fix), and plan how you’ll avoid it next time.

suggestions.title: CS 221 study sprint (lecture notes → practice problems)
suggestions.description: To support your 3.8 GPA goal, dedicate a focused sprint to CS 221: review the most recent lecture notes, then immediately work through related practice problems to lock in concepts.

suggestions.title: Study session
suggestions.description: Spend focused time studying on the most urgent school material or assignments.

behaviorCriteria: [Enrichment] Agent must create aspects for each distinct life area mentioned (school, work, hobbies, health)
[Enrichment] Agent must create recurring events with correct days/times when user shares schedule details
[Enrichment] Agent must NOT create duplicate aspects or events for the same activity
[Enrichment] Agent must ask about ALL life areas (school/work, health, personal, daily routine) across the conversation
[Enrichment] Agent must call tools SILENTLY -- no narrating "I will now create an aspect for..."
[Enrichment] Agent must keep responses to 2-4 sentences, no markdown, no bullet points
[Enrichment] Agent must create aspect FIRST, then event using that aspect (correct sequencing)
[Enrichment] Agent must capture goals mentioned by user via create_goal tool
[Enrichment] Agent must update profile with occupation, work hours, preferences when shared
[Enrichment] Agent should cover all 5 areas within the turn budget (school, work, health, personal, routine)
[Conversation] Schedule queries ("what do I have today") must call list_events or search_events, not create anything
[Conversation] Event creation must use the correct aspect for the activity type
[Conversation] Event modification must search for the existing event first, then update it -- not create a new one
[Conversation] Event deletion must confirm the right event before deleting (search first)
[Conversation] Agent must NOT create duplicate events when one already exists at that time
[Conversation] Agent must be aware of existing schedule when suggesting times ("do I have time for X")
[Conversation] Task creation should use create_task, not create_event for non-time-bound items
[Conversation] Goal check-ins should call list_goals or check_in_goal, not create new goals
[Conversation] Responses must be concise and natural -- no markdown headers, no numbered lists unless asked
[Conversation] Agent must not call unnecessary tools -- a simple greeting needs zero tool calls
[Conversation] Agent must handle ambiguous time references correctly ("tomorrow", "next Monday", "this afternoon")
[Conversation] When asked about free time, agent must call find_free_time and give specific windows
[Scheduler] Every suggestion must reference a specific goal, task, or aspect -- no generic filler
[Scheduler] Suggestion descriptions must explain WHY (reference the goal/task it advances)
[Scheduler] High-energy tasks (deep work, studying) must be placed in mornings/early afternoon
[Scheduler] Low-energy tasks (review, planning) must be placed in evenings
[Scheduler] No overlapping slots with existing events
[Scheduler] Minimum 60-minute duration for all slots
[Scheduler] Maximum 3-4 slots per day, spread across 7 days
[Scheduler] Multiple life areas must be represented (not all one aspect)
[Scheduler] Slots must have 30+ minute gaps between them and existing events
[Anti-pattern] DUPLICATE CREATION: Creating a second "CS 161 Lecture" when one already exists at MWF 10:30am
[Anti-pattern] TOOL NARRATION: "I will now create an aspect for your class" instead of just doing it
[Anti-pattern] GENERIC SUGGESTIONS: "Watch a TED talk" or "Go for a walk" with no goal connection
[Anti-pattern] OVER-TOOLING: Calling 5 tools when 1 would suffice
[Anti-pattern] UNDER-TOOLING: Responding to "add a meeting" with text instead of calling create_event
[Anti-pattern] WRONG TOOL: Using create_event for a task, or create_task for a scheduled meeting
[Anti-pattern] MARKDOWN IN CHAT: Using ## headers, **bold**, - bullet points in conversational responses
[Anti-pattern] IGNORING CONTEXT: Suggesting a meeting at 2pm when user has class then
[Anti-pattern] ASPECT ORPHANS: Creating events without linking to an aspect
[Anti-pattern] STALE RESPONSES: Not reflecting the current state of the calendar in answers
